headline: Van Jones reflects back on best advice from Amazons Jeff Bezos
summary: Listen and subscribe to Financial Freestyle on Apple Podcasts, Spotify, or wherever you find your favorite podcasts. Van Jones has received a lot of advice from top executives over the course of his career. In the latest episode of Financial Freestyle with Ross Mac, the CNN commentator and Dream Machine Innovation Lab founder shares his favorite piece of advice from Amazon (AMZN) founder and former CEO Jeff Bezos. Jones describes a conversation he had with the Amazon executive chairman at Bezoss ranch. He recalls Bezos saying: Every day is day one. I wake up every day as if I have nothing, as if Ive done nothing. What can I do today that will be impactful? Reflecting on the moment, Jones recalls his reaction: Sometimes dreams come true. He goes, No, they usually come true. Jones believes that people need to dream bigger. Usually you dream, Yeah, I hope I can pass this test...I hope I can pay my rent... and most of the time it happens, says Jones. He [Bezos] just had bigger dreams. Catch Van Joness full interview on the latest episode of Financial Freestyle with Ross Mac. Financial Freestyle with Ross Mac on Yahoo Finance is dedicated to promoting economic prosperity for all. Through expert insights, practical advice, and inspiring success stories, we empower you to build and grow wealth. Join us on this transformative journey toward financial freedom and inclusive economic growth. This post was written by Meredith Lawrence.

headline: PCE data, Big Tech earnings: What to Watch
summary: A pivotal week lies ahead for markets as several Big Tech heavyweights prepare to release their third quarter earnings reports, including Alphabet (GOOG, GOOGL), Meta (META), Microsoft (MSFT), Apple (AAPL), Amazon (AMZN), and Intel (INTC). On the economic calendar, Thursday, October 31 brings the release of the Personal Consumption Expenditures (PCE) index, the Federal Reserves preferred measure of inflation. This crucial data point will provide investors with fresh insights into potential monetary policy decisions. The week ends with Fridays Jobs Report, another critical economic indicator that could influence market sentiment and Fed policy outlook. To watch more expert insights and analysis on the latest market action, check out more Market Domination Overtime here. This post was written by Angel Smith
